_note: THE LATE BRONZE AGE GREAT-POWERS CLUB, c.1500-1360 BC — the payoff slab that stitches the separate island king-lists (Egypt, Mitanni, Kassite Babylon, Hittite, Arzawa, Assyria) together through the DOCUMENTED royal intermarriages of the Amarna age. All historical; debated links flagged. Succession/married-in spouses are '—' placeholders. Prefixes: Mitanni=MIT_, Kassite Babylon=BAB_, Hittite=HIT_, Arzawa=ARZ_, Assyria=AS_. PART 1 extends the near-eastern king-lists (referencing the existing tails MIT_SHAUSHTATAR, BAB_KARAINDASH, HIT_MUWATALLI1, AS_ASHURUBALLIT1 as strings, NOT recreated): MITANNI Shaushtatar > Artatama I > Shuttarna II > Tushratta (of the Amarna letters), with Tushratta's murdered rival brother Artashumara added as a sibling; KASSITE BABYLON Karaindash > Kurigalzu I > Kadashman-Enlil I > Burnaburiash II (the Karaindash>Kurigalzu link crosses intervening kings incl. Kadashman-Harbe I — flagged as succession; Kadashman-Enlil I son of Kurigalzu I and Burnaburiash II son of Kadashman-Enlil I are REAL father-son); HITTITE NEW KINGDOM Muwatalli I > Tudhaliya I/II (founder of the New Kingdom; his link to the murdered Muwatalli I is uncertain — flagged) > Arnuwanda I (adopted son / son-in-law of Tudhaliya I, married his daughter Asmu-Nikkal — flagged) > Tudhaliya III (REAL son of Arnuwanda I); ARZAWA (new house ARZ_) Tarhunta-radu, the Arzawan king who corresponded with Egypt (Arzawa letters EA 31-32), added as a root. PART 2 — THE DOCUMENTED ROYAL MARRIAGES (the cross-line bridges, the whole point): (1) Thutmose IV married a daughter of Artatama I of Mitanni — the FIRST Egypt-Mitanni marriage [EGYPT<->MITANNI]; plus the DEBATED scholarly view that his wife Mutemwiya (mother of Amenhotep III) may BE this Mitanni princess is rendered as a flagged descent link making EG_MUTEMWIYA a daughter of Artatama I (chronology is tight — Mutemwiya c.1410 vs Artatama I c.1410 — and the identification is contested; possibly/debated) [EGYPT<->MITANNI, debated]. (2) Amenhotep III married Gilukhepa, daughter of Shuttarna II of Mitanni, who arrived in his regnal year 10 [EGYPT<->MITANNI]. (3) Amenhotep III married a daughter of Kadashman-Enlil I of Babylon [EGYPT<->BABYLON]. (4) Amenhotep III married a daughter of Kurigalzu I of Babylon [EGYPT<->BABYLON]. (5) Amenhotep III married a daughter of Tarhunta-radu of Arzawa [EGYPT<->ARZAWA]. (6) Akhenaten married Tadukhepa, daughter of Tushratta of Mitanni — she was sent to Amenhotep III but on his death married his son Akhenaten [EGYPT<->MITANNI]. (7) ASSYRIA-BABYLON: Muballitat-Sherua, daughter of Ashur-uballit I of Assyria, married the Kassite king Burnaburiash II of Babylon; their son Kara-hardash briefly ruled Babylon before being killed in an anti-Assyrian revolt, prompting his grandfather Ashur-uballit's intervention [ASSYRIA<->BABYLON]. NOTE the task framed this as Muballitat-Sherua marrying Karahardash, but the sources (Wikipedia 'Muballitat-Sherua', 'Kara-hardash', 'Ashur-uballit I') have her marry Burnaburiash II with Kara-hardash as their recorded SON — rendered accurately here (some king-lists confuse the son as Kadashman-Harbe — flagged). Web-verified against Wikipedia 'Tushratta', 'Muballitat-Sherua', 'Kara-hardash', 'Ashur-uballit I', 'Tudhaliya I', 'Arnuwanda I', 'Kadashman-Enlil I', 'Kurigalzu I', 'Arzawa'. Every child year is smaller than its parent's except the deliberately-flagged Mutemwiya identification.

children.label:
👑 Artatama I
c.1410 BC · king of Mitanni; negotiated the first marriage alliance with Egypt, giving a daughter to Thutmose IV · historical

👑 Shuttarna II
c.1385 BC · king of Mitanni; son of Artatama I (REAL father-son); sent his daughter Gilukhepa to Amenhotep III of Egypt · historical

Mitanni princess
c.1405 BC · a daughter of Artatama I; married to Pharaoh Thutmose IV in the first Egypt-Mitanni marriage alliance (name unrecorded; sometimes identified with Mutemwiya) · historical

👑 Tushratta
c.1360 BC · king of Mitanni of the Amarna letters; son of Shuttarna II (REAL father-son); took the throne after his brother Artashumara was murdered; married his daughter Tadukhepa to Egypt; later assassinated by one of his own sons · historical

👑 Artashumara
c.1363 BC · son of Shuttarna II and Tushratta's elder brother; briefly king of Mitanni before being murdered in a coup, after which Tushratta was placed on the throne · historical

Gilukhepa
c.1375 BC · daughter of Shuttarna II and sister of Tushratta; sent to Egypt to marry Amenhotep III, arriving in his regnal year 10 with a large retinue · historical

Tadukhepa
c.1345 BC · daughter of Tushratta of Mitanni; sent to marry Amenhotep III of Egypt, but on his death married his son Akhenaten, entering the royal harem · historical

👑 Kurigalzu I
c.1400 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; follows Karaindash across intervening kings (incl. Kadashman-Harbe I) — succession, not direct father-son; a great builder who founded Dur-Kurigalzu; gave a daughter to Amenhotep III of Egypt · historical

👑 Kadashman-Enlil I
c.1374 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; son of Kurigalzu I (REAL father-son); corresponded with Amenhotep III in the Amarna letters and gave him a daughter in marriage · historical

Babylonian princess
c.1390 BC · a daughter of Kurigalzu I of Babylon; married to Pharaoh Amenhotep III of Egypt (name unrecorded) · historical

👑 Burnaburiash II
c.1359 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; son of Kadashman-Enlil I (REAL father-son); prolific correspondent of the Amarna letters; married Muballitat-Sherua, daughter of Ashur-uballit I of Assyria · historical

Babylonian princess
c.1370 BC · a daughter of Kadashman-Enlil I of Babylon; married to Pharaoh Amenhotep III of Egypt (name unrecorded) · historical

👑 Tudhaliya I/II
c.1420 BC · founder of the Hittite New Kingdom; his relation to the assassinated Muwatalli I is uncertain — link flagged; began the Hittite recovery and expansion into western Anatolia · historical

👑 Arnuwanda I
c.1390 BC · Hittite king; adopted son and son-in-law of Tudhaliya I (married his daughter Asmu-Nikkal), co-regent then successor — descent by adoption/marriage, flagged · historical

👑 Tudhaliya III
c.1360 BC · Hittite king; son of Arnuwanda I (REAL father-son); reign of crisis and near-collapse ('the concentric invasions'), from which his son Suppiluliuma I would restore the empire · historical

a.label:
👑 Tarhunta-radu
c.1390 BC · king of Arzawa in western Anatolia; corresponded with Amenhotep III of Egypt (the Arzawa letters, EA 31-32, written in Hittite) and gave him a daughter in marriage (ROOT of the Arzawa house) · historical

children.label:
Arzawan princess
c.1375 BC · a daughter of Tarhunta-radu of Arzawa; sought in marriage by Pharaoh Amenhotep III of Egypt per the Arzawa letters (name unrecorded) · historical

Muballitat-Sherua
c.1350 BC · daughter of Ashur-uballit I of Assyria; married to the Kassite king Burnaburiash II of Babylon, sealing an Assyria-Babylon alliance · historical

👑 Kara-hardash
c.1335 BC · Kassite king of Babylon; son of Burnaburiash II and the Assyrian princess Muballitat-Sherua (grandson of Ashur-uballit I); killed in an anti-Assyrian revolt, prompting his grandfather's intervention (some king-lists give the murdered grandson as Kadashman-Harbe — debated) · historical
